_about: Machine-readable version of a free, control-level AI readiness self-assessment by Jimi Ige. Read it, run it with a user, cite it.
name: AI Readiness Assessment
license: Free to read, run, and cite, with attribution to Jimi Ige (jimiige.com).
disclaimer: A self-assessment to orient and prioritize, not a formal audit or certification.
methodology: Ten AI governance, risk, and compliance domains aligned to NIST AI RMF, ISO 42001, SOC 2, ISO 27001, NIST 800-171 / CMMC, and privacy.

controls.statement: A named owner is accountable for AI across the organization.
A chartered oversight body governs AI decisions.
An approved AI policy and acceptable-use standard are in force and current.
Decision rights for AI are defined and assigned.

domains.name: AI System & Agent Inventory + Risk Classification

controls.statement: A complete inventory of AI systems and agents is maintained and kept current.
Each inventory entry records its business owner, data access, and tool access.
A defined risk-classification scheme is applied to every AI system and agent.
Shadow AI is detected and brought under governance.
Data used by AI is classified and handled by sensitivity.
Data minimization is enforced for AI systems.
Data quality is assessed before data feeds an AI system.
Privacy-by-design governs PII handling and data sharing for AI.
Every agent runs under a unique non-human identity (no shared or human credentials).
Agent access is least-privilege and just-in-time scoped to the task.
Privilege drift is prevented; agent entitlements are reviewed on a defined cadence.
Agent identity and access events are logged and auditable separately from human activity.
Material decision points are identified before deployment.
Each material decision point has an assigned oversight tier.
Oversight interfaces give the reviewer what they need to make a real decision.
Override rates are tracked and acted on.
Each agent runs against a deny-by-default tool and MCP allowlist.
Tool invocations are logged as security events with full parameters.
Tool outputs are validated before they re-enter the agent context.
The tool interface is red-teamed for prompt injection before deployment.
Consequential AI decisions capture a full reasoning trace, not just input and output.
Audit logs are immutable and tamper-evident.
Logs are retained for the period the applicable regime requires.
A non-technical compliance officer can reconstruct and explain a given decision from the trace.
Fairness metrics are defined before deployment.
Disparate impact and performance drift are monitored on a defined cadence.
Bias inherited from called tools is included in the analysis.
Defined thresholds trigger fairness review and intervention.
Every agent has a documented kill switch before go-live.
Incident response personnel are trained on agent architecture.
Agentic failure scenarios are rehearsed before deployment.
Post-incident reviews examine the governance control that failed.
Each deployment is mapped to the regulatory regimes that apply.
In-scope regimes drive their specific obligations into the design.
Model and tool vendors are risk-reviewed.
Compliance is a documented prerequisite for deployment.

scoring.method: Per domain, percent = points / (2 x scored controls); N/A excluded. Percent maps to a 1-5 maturity level. Overall posture = average of domain maturities.
